search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG95 stable chest pain follow-up history tests patient information
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic stress testing, medicine, or referral plan
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Non-acute status and a workflow label do not establish a diagnosis or present safety.
Testing and treatment remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with recent chest pain or discomfort undergoing non-acute clinician follow-up.
setting_applicability: Primary or outpatient follow-up; recurrent current or severe symptoms require direct acute assessment.
UAE_applicability: International NICE evidence requires UAE emergency, diagnostic, referral, and facility adaptation; DHA chest-pain evidence remains separately registered.
recency_verification: NICE CG95 last updated 2016-11-30 was reviewed on 2026-07-13; major recency gap remains.
superseded_check: CG95 remains current NICE guidance but is old; no treatment content was mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE stable chest-pain section supports documenting interval symptoms, function, associated features, cardiovascular risk, prior investigations, examination, and clinician-reviewed results without inferring stable angina or another diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The information section supports documenting patient questions, clinician explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, recurrent-pain safety advice, and arranged follow-up without generating advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

unresolved_source_gaps: Current pain status, pattern and triggers, associated features, risk, prior diagnosis and tests, examination, ECG, biomarkers, clinician interpretation, treatment, referral, safety-net content, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
